purpose: RED state at HEAD pre-M3 (substring scanner active, AST detection only on Python path)

string_literal_fp_tests.explanation: Each FP failure represents the closes-#24 root pattern: substring scanner matches source/sink names inside string literals or comments. M3 (canonical compute_taint_with_tree dispatch) flips these RED→GREEN.

expected_red_state.positive_tests: Most should PASS at HEAD (substring scanner catches them). A handful may FAIL where source/sink are on different lines or the patterns aren't substring-matched today — those become GREEN after M2/M3 AST extension.
expected_red_state.string_literal_fp_tests: Most FAIL on the 14 fall-through langs (FP class active). Python tests pass because analyze_python_file already uses tree-sitter. Some fixtures may have ambiguous patterns and pass accidentally — that's not a problem; the M5 binary verification gates on the canonical /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/ corpus.
expected_red_state.composite: FAIL at HEAD (substring scanner emits ≥1 finding from string literal); GREEN after M3.
